mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly depressing S&P 500 valuations and generating negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one trading week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp, or signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal income and housing starts indicate a synchronized collapse in consumer spending and construction demand, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter as corporate revenue forecasts are downgraded.
failure_condition: The joint decline of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative 60-day returns, with a bivariate regression coefficient statistically indistinguishable from zero over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth during a post-recession recovery phase signals expanding corporate output capacity and rising profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative or flat 20-day forward returns during recovery phases, or the mechanism predictive power vanishes when restricted to post-recession samples.

mechanism_narrative: Strong quarterly GDP growth exceeding 3.0% annualized confirms robust aggregate demand, prompting immediate equity market repricing and positive S&P 500 returns within one trading week.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns are negative in > 60% of quarters where GDPC1 growth surpasses 3.0%, or the threshold effect disappears in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: High real interest rates, defined as the federal funds rate exceeding core inflation by more than 1.5 percentage points, suppress economic activity and corporate investment, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The real rate spread fails to predict negative 60-day returns, with out-of-sample R-squared below 0.01 or positive mean returns when the spread exceeds 1.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls during a low-inflation environment signals healthy wage-driven consumption without triggering aggressive central bank tightening, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth generates negative 20-day forward returns when inflation is below 2.5%, or the signal Rank IC turns negative in low-inflation subsamples.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive months of industrial production contraction signal imminent supply-side weakness and falling corporate revenues, triggering immediate risk aversion and negative S&P 500 returns within one trading week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 50% of instances following two consecutive months of INDPRO decline, or the threshold signal shows no statistical edge in out-of-sample testing.
